senses.gloss: to push someone away from something
to touch and injure someone in the eye to the point of making tears flow

body.text: He looked from the outer angle of the eye:
he put the edge of his eyelid in motion,
in a state of commotion, and looked:
the putting the eyelids in motion,
in a state of commotion, in looking:
raised, and he does not put his eyelids in motion,
does not twinkle with his eye,
, aor. and inf. n. as above, meaning
became in a state of commotion:
, (O, K, TA, and so in a copy of the S,) or
, (so in one of my copies of the S,) aor. and inf. n. as above, [
he closed one of his eyelids upon the other:
he put his eyelids in motion,
his eyelids were put in motion
in a state of commotion, by looking.
said of the eye, will be found below.]
in a state of commotion, with looking,
(O, K, TA,) or (O, in the K erroneously “ and, ” TA)
(Er-Rághib, Msb, TA) because the putting in motion of the eyelid constantly attends that act: (Er-Rághib, TA:) and
. (Ham p. 111.) It is said in the Kur [xiv. 44]
Their look shall not revert to them;
shall not be withdrawn by them
from that upon which they shall look]. (S, O.) And in the same [xxvii. 40],

body.text: I will bring it to thee before thy look
therefrom: or,] accord. to Fr, meaning
before a thing shall be brought to thee
from the extent of thy vision:
in the space in which thou shalt open thine eye and then close it:
in the space in which one shall reach the extent of thy vision.
Such a one looked with a furtive glance
contracted his eyelids over the main portion of his eye and looked with the rest of it,
by reason of shyness or fear. (Har p. 565.) And

body.text: It was the case that when any one of his family had a complaint, the cooking-pot did not cease to be on the fire but he arrived at one of his two limits
because these are the two terminations of the case of the diseased. (O, K.) ―         -b8-  And one says,
He will not have control over his mouth and his anus:
referring to him who has drunk medicine or become intoxicated. (AO, ISk, S, O, K.) ―         -b9-  And
Such a one is corrupt in respect of the tongue and the
does not, know which of his two extremities is the longer,
is used as signifying (assumed tropical:)  the
(TA:) or the meaning is, as some say, (assumed tropical:)
which of his two halves is the longer;
the lower or the upper: (TA:) or (assumed tropical:)
the lineage of his father or that of his mother
(O, TA:) i. e., which of his two parents is the more generous, or noble: so says Fr. (TA.) ―         -b11-
in respect of male and female ancestors.
any relations whom it is unlawful for him to marry.
the learned men, of the earth,

. (O, See the latter of these words.) And hence, as some explain it, the saying in the Kur [xiii. 41, like one in xxi. 45],

body.text: Have they not seen that we visit,
bring destruction upon, the land, curtailing it of its learned men?
]; the meaning being, the death of its learned men: (O, TA:) or, as some say, [
curtailing it of its inhabitants and its fruits;
for they say that] the meaning is, the death of its inhabitants and the diminution of its fruits: (TA:) or it means,
one by one: (Az, O, L:) Ibn-'Arafeh says that the meaning is, we lay open by conquest, to the Prophet, (
The lower orders of the people:
but this I believe to be post-classical.] ―         -b15-

but is perhaps post-classical.] ―         -b18-  And you say,
